response.justification: Candidate is a Senior/Staff-level engineer with 15+ years of experience applying for an entry-level Junior Java Developer role — a severe seniority mismatch that creates high flight-risk and compensation-expectation gaps. While technically very strong, the resume shows minimal recent Java work (primarily TypeScript, Node.js, DLang), only basic German (role requires strong German), and no insurance-sector experience. The role is designed to develop entry-level talent, which this candidate has long outgrown.
strengths: 15+ years of polyglot software development with strong craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD, pairing)
Berlin-based with proven agile, client-facing, and architecture experience
Demonstrated teaching/mentoring track record (workshops, dojos, Selenium course)
concerns: Massive seniority mismatch — Staff Engineer applying for entry-level Junior role
German listed as only 'Basic' while role requires strong written and verbal German
Java is a peripheral skill; recent stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, not Java

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering and agile background
